rag_rules: Generate assume/guarantee clauses only for visible ports or constants explicitly defined in the AADL model, using direct references without qualification.
Place component-type contracts in component type annexes; implementation bindings (assign) belong only in implementation annexes.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees must reflect requirement-bound numeric ranges or Boolean relations using only model-exposed, typed identifiers.
Use eq declarations to define reusable Boolean expressions for ranges or conditions, ensuring type consistency with port declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use syntax: annex agree {** ... **}; with no trailing semicolon inside the block.
Constant declarations must use 'const name : type = value;' and expressions must return bool for guarantees/assumptions.
anti_patterns: Do not infer numeric bounds (e.g., x >= 0) unless explicitly stated in the requirement.
Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) or event ports as numeric/Boolean variables in AGREE expressions.
Do not use subcomponent paths or package-qualified names unless explicitly visible and declared in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement decision for implementation vs type
